title: Support Aisha's university tuition
story:
My name is Aisha Rahman, and I am a second-year student studying Biomedical Engineering at the University of Sheffield, United Kingdom. I moved from Lahore, Pakistan in September 2023 on a student visa after securing conditional admission. My father, Tariq Rahman, worked as a secondary school teacher in Lahore and took out a personal loan of PKR 450,000 to cover my first-year tuition of £9,250 and initial living costs.

In March 2025, my father suffered a cardiac event that left him unable to work for an extended period. His employer, Government Boys Higher Secondary School No. 4 in Gulberg, placed him on unpaid medical leave effective 15 March 2025. Without his monthly salary of PKR 65,000, the family has no steady income. My mother, Rukhsana Begum, runs a small tailoring business from home generating roughly PKR 18,000 per month — far short of what is needed to service the existing loan, cover household expenses for my two younger siblings, and pay my upcoming fees.

My second-year tuition invoice from the University of Sheffield (Student ID: 230847192) is due on 14 July 2025 for the amount of £9,250. The University's Student Funding Office has confirmed in writing that I am ineligible for the tuition fee loan as an international student. If payment is not received by the deadline, my enrolment will be suspended and my Tier-4 visa will be at risk of curtailment.

I have exhausted all available departmental bursaries and applied unsuccessfully to three charitable foundations. I am seeking £9,250 to cover the Year 2 tuition fee in full. Every pound raised goes directly to the University's fee account — I will publish the payment confirmation receipt once processed. My studies, if completed, lead to a career where I intend to serve underserved communities in both the UK and Pakistan. This campaign is my last viable option to remain enrolled.
vouching_narrative: Dr Nadia Hussain, Senior Lecturer in Biomedical Engineering at the University of Sheffield (email: n.hussain@sheffield.ac.uk, staff directory: https://www.sheffield.ac.uk/bmbe/staff/nadia-hussain), has verbally confirmed to me that Aisha is a registered student in good academic standing and that her circumstances as described are known to the department. Additionally, Imam Khalid Farooqi of Sheffield Islamic Society (Wolseley Road Mosque, S9 3RB) has agreed to write a letter of support upon request — he knows the family through their attendance at the Sheffield Pakistani Community Centre events since Aisha's arrival in 2023.
